title: Add a Robot From the Robot Selection Screen
summary: Use the Robot Selection screen in the web application or Geek Plus RMS view to add a robot by entering its robot ID, clicking Add, and confirming the system reports the add was successful. The training frames this as a support-oriented action used during troubleshooting.
when_to_use: Use when support personnel need to add a robot from the Robot Selection screen in the web application or Geek Plus RMS view, including troubleshooting situations described in the training.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to decide the correct circumstances for putting a robot back into the system; the source states that the correct way to put it back is covered later.
Do not assume this procedure provides criteria for when a robot should be added; the source segment primarily shows how to perform the add action.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the Robot Selection screen, enter the robot ID, perform the Add action, and verify the system reports success.

safety_notes: Use only authorized access to the web application or Geek Plus RMS view.
Remote access to the RMS view depends on authorization for UPS VPN through Zscaler.
The source frames add/remove robot actions as support mechanics used during troubleshooting.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the web application or Geek Plus RMS view
Device connected to the OT network or authorized remote access through UPS VPN and Z Scaler
Robot ID for the robot being added

access_required: Access to the web application or Geek Plus RMS view
Device on the OT network, or authorized remote access through UPS VPN and Zscaler

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the Robot Selection interface with Add Robot and Remove Robot procedures, including robot ID entry, Add button, and success confirmation.

steps.title: Open the Robot Selection screen
steps.purpose: Reach the interface where the Add Robot action is performed.
steps.instruction: Open the web application or Geek Plus RMS view that shows the Robot Selection screen. Use a device on the OT network, or authorized remote access if available.
steps.expected_result: The Robot Selection screen is visible in the web application or RMS view.
steps.healthy_condition: The support user can access the Robot Selection view from an OT-network-connected device, an authorized remote session, or an available HMI station.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot access the Robot Selection screen or does not have authorized connectivity.

actions.label: Open RMS or web application
actions.exact_action: Open the web application or RMS view that shows the Robot Selection screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection screen in the web application / Geek Plus RMS view.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if OT network access is unavailable and remote access is not authorized.
Stop or escalate if the Robot Selection screen cannot be reached.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Any device connected on the OT network, whether a laptop or computer, will have access to the web software application through that IP.
Remote support will not have access to OT net unless authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler; after authorization, the site address or RMS address can be used to access Geek Plus RMS.
The shop floor has two HMI stations where this view is displayed for the local team, with one located at the hospital.

steps.title: Locate the Add Robot section
steps.purpose: Identify the correct input field and control used to add a robot.
steps.instruction: Locate the Add Robot section on the Robot Selection screen, including the robot ID input field and the Add button.
steps.expected_result: The Add Robot input field and Add button are identified on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Robot Selection screen clearly shows the Add Robot area with the robot entry field and Add control.
steps.failure_condition: The Add Robot section is not visible or the user cannot identify the correct field and button.

actions.label: Find Add Robot controls
actions.exact_action: Locate the Add Robot section on the Robot Selection screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add Robot input field and Add button in the Robot Selection area.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Add Robot controls are not present on the Robot Selection screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Training slide showing the web application steps to add or remove a robot by robot ID in the Robot Selection interface.
Robot Selection screen in the web application supports Add Robot and Remove Robot.

steps.title: Enter the robot ID
steps.purpose: Specify which robot should be added.
steps.instruction: Enter the corresponding robot ID in the Add Robot field.
steps.expected_result: The target robot ID is entered in the Add Robot field.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended robot ID appears in the field.
steps.failure_condition: The robot ID is unavailable, cannot be entered, or does not correspond to the intended robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot ID entry field under the Add Robot section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the correct robot ID is not known.
Stop or escalate if the field does not accept the robot ID.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Add Robot: 1. Enter the corresponding robot ID.
Robot add and remove functions use robot ID entry.

steps.title: Click Add
steps.purpose: Submit the add request for the specified robot.
steps.expected_result: The system processes the add request for the entered robot ID.
steps.healthy_condition: The Add action is accepted by the interface.
steps.failure_condition: The Add action cannot be triggered or the interface does not respond.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add button in the Robot Selection Add Robot section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Add button is unavailable or does not respond.

steps.title: Verify successful add confirmation
steps.purpose: Confirm the system accepted and completed the add action.
steps.instruction: Verify that the system shows the adding was successful.
steps.expected_result: The system displays a successful add confirmation.
steps.healthy_condition: A success indication is shown for the add action.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not show that adding was successful.

actions.label: Confirm success message
actions.exact_action: Verify that the system shows that the adding was successful.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Success confirmation associated with the Add Robot action.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system does not show that adding was successful.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Add Robot: 3. The system will show that the adding was successful.

source_evidence.candidate_note: Escalate if the system does not show that adding was successful.

steps.title: Use matching robot lookup if partial entry returns results
steps.purpose: Support robot identification when the system shows matching robots for partial entry.
steps.instruction: If the robot has already been introduced in the system, type identifying ending digits in the robot field and select a matching robot if the system shows matching results.
steps.expected_result: A matching robot can be identified from the field results when partial ending digits are entered.
steps.healthy_condition: Matching robot results appear and the intended robot can be selected from the displayed matches.
steps.failure_condition: No matching results appear when expected, or the intended robot cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Enter partial robot digits
actions.exact_action: Type identifying ending digits in the robot field.

actions.label: Select matching robot
actions.exact_action: Select a matching robot if the system shows matching results.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot field used for ID entry; use it to observe whether matching robot results appear.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if multiple matches appear and the correct robot cannot be identified.
Stop or escalate if expected matching results do not appear.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The training notes that the field below Robot accepts the robot identifier, and if a robot is already introduced, partial ending digits can display matching robots.

source_evidence.extraction_note: Included as a source-backed optional lookup behavior rather than a required branch.

success_criteria: The specified robot is added in the web application.
The system displays a successful add confirmation.
healthy_conditions: Add Robot field and Add button are visible.
The correct robot ID can be entered or matched.
The system shows that adding was successful.
failure_conditions: Robot Selection screen cannot be accessed.
Add Robot controls are not available.
The correct robot ID cannot be entered or identified.
The system does not show that adding was successful.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the system does not show that adding was successful.
Escalate if authorized access to the Robot Selection screen is not available.
Escalate if the correct robot cannot be confidently identified from the robot ID field or matching results.
Do not infer the correct circumstances for adding a robot back; the source says the correct way to put it back is covered later.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection screen with Add Robot workflow, robot ID field, Add button, and success confirmation.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Slide shows Robot Selection with Add Robot and Remove Robot actions using robot ID entry and success confirmation.
Add Robot: 1. Enter the corresponding robot ID. 2. Click "Add". 3. The system will show that the adding was successful.

missing_details: The source does not provide a specific success message text.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing the procedure.
The source does not define whether production must be stopped before performing this action.
The source does not define LOTO requirements for this action.
The source does not provide exact navigation steps to reach the Robot Selection screen beyond accessing the web application or RMS view.
The source does not provide decision criteria for when a robot should be added back; it states that topic is covered later.
